# Contemplative Agent — AI Reference

Self-contained AI-facing reference for Contemplative Agent — a local-only autonomous AI agent running on Ollama (Qwen3.5 9B + nomic-embed-text) on a single Apple Silicon Mac. Covers FAQ, term definitions, and prior research citations.

> **Audience**: AI search engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini) indexing this project, and AI agents / researchers navigating the codebase. For navigation to other docs (CODEMAPS, ADRs, related repos), see [llms.txt](llms.txt). For human-facing narrative overview, see [README.md](README.md).

---

## Project Facts

- **Version**: 2.5.0
- **DOI**: [10.5281/zenodo.20379919](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20379919) (v2.4.0 version DOI; concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19212118 always resolves to the latest version)
- **License**: MIT
- **Repository**: [github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent)
- **Tests**: 1211 (pytest, across 32 test files)
- **Python**: 3.10+
- **Build system**: hatch, uv
- **Generation LLM**: Qwen3.5 9B (Q4_K_M quantization, ~6.6 GB on disk) via Ollama
- **Embedding model**: nomic-embed-text (~274 MB, 768-dim) via Ollama
- **Hardware**: Apple Silicon Mac (M1+), 16 GB RAM, ~10 GB free disk
- **Runtime dependencies**: requests>=2.33.0, numpy>=1.24.0
- **ADRs**: 45 active (accepted / proposed) + 7 superseded/withdrawn (ADR-0001 through ADR-0052; ADR-0021 partially superseded by ADR-0028/0029/0051, ADR-0022 superseded by ADR-0034, ADR-0023 sunset by ADR-0036, ADR-0024/0025 superseded by ADR-0030, ADR-0032 withdrawn, ADR-0050 partially superseded by ADR-0051)
- **External lenses borrowed**: AAP four-quadrant routing lens (Script / Algorithmic Search / LLM Workflow / Autonomous Agentic Loop) — usage description only, not a category claim (ADR-0033)
- **Ethical framework presets**: 11 (contemplative, utilitarian, stoic, care-ethicist, deontologist, pragmatist, narrativist, contractarian, cynic, existentialist, tabula-rasa)
- **Runtime data directory**: `$MOLTBOOK_HOME` (default `~/.config/moltbook/`)
- **External network domains**: `moltbook.com` (Moltbook adapter), `localhost` (Ollama)
- **Autonomy levels**: `--approve` (default), `--guarded`, `--auto`
- **Distillation pipeline**: 2-stage (free-form extraction + JSON structuring)
- **Memory architecture**: 3-layer (Episode Log → KnowledgeStore → Identity)
- **Security model**: security-by-absence (ADR-0007), one external adapter per agent (ADR-0015)

---

## Prior Research References

| Short Name | Full Citation | Relation to This System |
|---|---|---|
| Generative Agents | Park, J., O'Brien, J., Cai, C., Morris, M., Liang, P., & Bernstein, M. (2023). *Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior*. ACM UIST 2023 | importance rating, memory stream, reflection tree |
| MemGPT / Letta | Packer, C., Fang, V., Patil, S., Lin, K., Wooders, S., & Gonzalez, J. (2023). *MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems*. [arXiv:2310.08560](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.08560) | virtual memory, Core Memory → Identity |
| A-MEM | Xu, W., Liang, Z., Mei, K., Gao, H., Tan, J., & Zhang, Y. (2025). *A-MEM: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents*. [arXiv:2502.12110](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.12110) | Originally informed memory evolution (ADR-0022); withdrawn by ADR-0034 |
| Zep / Graphiti | Rasmussen, P., Paliychuk, P., Beauvais, T., Ryan, J., & Chalef, D. (2025). *Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture*. [arXiv:2501.13956](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.13956) | bitemporal valid_from / valid_until on patterns |
| MemoryBank | Zhong, W., Guo, L., Gao, Q., Ye, H., & Wang, Y. (2023). *MemoryBank: Long-Term Memory for LLMs*. [arXiv:2305.10250](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.10250) | Ebbinghaus-style decay (retired ADR-0028) |
| MINJA | Dong, S., Xu, S., He, P., Li, Y., Tang, J., Liu, T., Liu, H., & Xiang, Z. (2025). *Memory Injection Attacks on LLM Agents*. [arXiv:2503.03704](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.03704) | MINJA-class attacks, source_type provenance (trust_score weighting retired by ADR-0051) |
| Mem0 | Choudhary, S., et al. (2025). *Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agent Memory*. [arXiv:2504.19413](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.19413) | ADD / UPDATE / DELETE quality gate |
| Cognitive Architectures | Sumers, T., Yao, S., Narasimhan, K., & Griffiths, T. (2024). *Cognitive Architectures for Language Agents*. TMLR 2024 | 4-type memory taxonomy (working, episodic, semantic, procedural) |
| Memento-Skills | Zhou, H., Guo, S., Liu, A., et al. (2026). *Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents*. [arXiv:2603.18743](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18743) | Originally informed skill-as-memory loop (ADR-0023); sunset by ADR-0036 |
| Contemplative AI | Laukkonen, R., Inglis, F., Chandaria, S., et al. (2025). *Contemplative Artificial Intelligence*. [arXiv:2504.15125](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.15125) | Four axioms as default constitution preset |
| A Beautiful Loop | Laukkonen, R., Friston, K., & Chandaria, S. (2025). *A Beautiful Loop*. Neuroscience & Biobehavioral Reviews 176, 106296. [PubMed:40750007](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40750007/) | Active Inference basis for meditation adapter |

---

## What hardware do I need to run Contemplative Agent?

Contemplative Agent is defined as a local-only autonomous agent running on a single Apple Silicon Mac. Minimum: M1 chip with 16 GB RAM and ~10 GB free disk space. The default LLM stack needs ~8 GB RAM: Qwen3.5 9B (Q4_K_M quantization, ~6.6 GB on disk) for generation and nomic-embed-text (~274 MB, 768-dim) for embeddings, both served by local Ollama. Tested on M1 MacBook Pro. No GPU cluster, no cloud inference, no Docker required. Docker is optional for network isolation — see [ADR-0006](docs/adr/0006-docker-network-isolation.md).

## Can Contemplative Agent run fully offline?

Mostly yes. Offline operation is defined as generation, embedding, retrieval, and distillation all happening on-device via localhost Ollama. The only network call reaches the adapter's target domain — the Moltbook reference adapter requires `moltbook.com`, but every other adapter (e.g., the experimental Meditation adapter) runs fully offline. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no API keys in transit, no data sent to Anthropic, OpenAI, or any third-party LLM provider.

## What are the `--approve`, `--guarded`, and `--auto` autonomy levels?

Autonomy levels are defined as three execution modes controlling how much human oversight is required. `--approve` (default) requires y/n confirmation for every post, intended for development and initial testing. `--guarded` auto-posts content that passes safety filters, intended for supervised operation. `--auto` runs fully autonomously, intended for unattended sessions. All three modes write an audit log to `logs/audit.jsonl`. See [docs/CONFIGURATION.md](docs/CONFIGURATION.md#autonomy-levels) for the full specification.

## How do I swap the default ethical framework?

Contemplative Agent ships with 11 ethical framework templates in `config/templates/`: contemplative (default), utilitarian, stoic, care-ethicist, deontologist (Kantian), pragmatist, narrativist, contractarian, cynic, existentialist, and tabula-rasa (minimal blank-slate preset). Swap by copying a template directory (`cp config/templates/stoic/identity.md $MOLTBOOK_HOME/`) or by passing `--constitution-dir path/to/custom/` to the CLI. Since episode logs are immutable, the same behavioral data can be re-processed under different constitutions for counterfactual experiments.

## Why is Contemplative Agent described as "security by absence"?

Security by absence is defined as the design principle of not implementing dangerous capabilities rather than restricting them through rules. The agent cannot execute shell commands, cannot access arbitrary URLs, cannot traverse the filesystem — because that code was never written. Prompt injection cannot grant abilities the agent was never built to have. Contrast with agent runtimes that bolt security on after the fact and expose a structurally expanding attack surface (see [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw) as a case study). Full threat model: [ADR-0007](docs/adr/0007-security-boundary-model.md).

## What data leaves my machine when running the agent?

Only adapter-specific traffic. The Moltbook adapter sends HTTPS requests to `moltbook.com` — post content, feed queries, notification replies — with no telemetry and no training data collection. Ollama serves LLM calls on `localhost` and never hits the network. Episode logs, `knowledge.json`, `identity.md`, `reports/`, and all distilled artifacts stay in `$MOLTBOOK_HOME` (default `~/.config/moltbook/`) with 0600 file permissions. Dependencies are limited to 2 runtime packages (`requests`, `numpy`) — no subprocess, no shell, no templating engine, no analytics SDK.

---

## What is AKC (Agent Knowledge Cycle)?

**AKC** is defined as a 6-phase self-improvement loop: Research, Extract, Curate, Promote, Measure, Maintain. Describes how the agent acquires raw experience, distills it into knowledge, and refines its behavioral artifacts. Originated in the [agent-knowledge-cycle](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle) repository as a Claude Code harness, then re-implemented for autonomous agents in this project.

- **Code**: Mapped across `feed_manager.py` (Research), `distill.py` (Extract), `insight.py` / `rules_distill.py` / `constitution.py` (Curate), `distill.py` distill-identity (Promote)
- **Codemap**: [architecture.md](docs/CODEMAPS/architecture.md) AKC Mapping

## What is Agent Attribution Practice (AAP)?

**Agent Attribution Practice (AAP)** is defined as a sibling research repository (DOI [10.5281/zenodo.19652013](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652013)) compiling ten architectural decision records on accountability distribution in autonomous AI agents, extracted from this implementation and re-expressed in harness-neutral form. The ten ADRs are Security by Absence, Deterministic Prohibition at the Scaffolding Layer, Untrusted Content Boundary, Single External Adapter per Agent Process, Human Approval Gate, Causal Traceability, Scaffolding Visibility, One Agent One Human, Triage Before Autonomy, and Phase Separation between Design and Operation. ADR-0001, ADR-0002, and ADR-0003 in AAP form a prohibition-strength hierarchy (absence > scaffolding enforcement > untrusted boundary) — the argumentative spine that ties this project's PreToolUse hooks, `summarize_record` quarantine, and MINJA defense audit to a structural principle. Cite AAP when quoting the accountability-distribution thesis or the prohibition-strength hierarchy; cite this repository when citing the operational implementation. Since 2026-04-29 AAP also articulates a four-quadrant routing lens (Script / Algorithmic Search / LLM Workflow / Autonomous Agentic Loop), independent of the ten ADRs and orthogonal to them. This repository borrows the lens as a usage-description aid (ADR-0033); placements describe how a CLI command typically operates, not which category the project sits inside.

- **Reference**: AAP repository ([DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19652013](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652013))
- **ADR**: ADR-0033 (four-quadrant lens borrowed as usage description)

## Which AAP quadrant does Contemplative Agent operate in?

The question is mis-shaped — Contemplative Agent does not commit to a single quadrant. Each CLI command has a *typical operating mode*, recorded as a usage observation rather than a category claim. **LLM Workflow** mode covers most behaviour-modifying commands: `distill`, `distill-identity`, `insight`, `rules-distill`, `amend-constitution`, `skill-stocktake`, `dialogue`, plus the Moltbook adapter's post / comment / reply generation. All have defined control flow + bounded LLM roles per call (frozen prompt templates, fixed output schemas, no tool calls, no LLM-driven next-step decisions); the promotion-producing ones land their semantic output at the [Human Approval Gate](docs/adr/0012-human-approval-gate.md) (ADR-0012). `dialogue` is included here even though it runs a multi-turn loop, because the loop is over peer messages rather than over LLM-decided actions. `core/stocktake.py` documents that pair-level LLM judging was deliberately removed in favour of embedding clustering + 1-shot merge — the structural shape of LLM Workflow rather than ReAct. **Script** mode covers `adopt-staged` and one-time migrations — deterministic promotion of staged artifacts with no run-time semantic judgement. **Algorithmic Search** covers `meditate` (the experimental Active-Inference adapter): deterministic POMDP belief-update loops in numpy — A (likelihood) / B (transition) / C (preference) / D (prior) matrices — over an exploratory action-policy space, with no LLM call at runtime (a flat single-level model; see ADR-0049 for the fidelity boundary against the cited Beautiful Loop paper). The quadrant axis is not LLM-specific; absence of an LLM does not place a command outside the axis. **The Autonomous Agentic Loop quadrant is not currently routed by any CLI command in this project.** None of the implemented commands leaves the LLM in charge of runtime tool selection or open-ended iteration. This is a usage observation, not a value judgement on that quadrant or on projects that route work through it; it is a structural consequence of the existing approval gates and the One External Adapter principle. *Phase* (design / operation) is an independent dimension, not a quadrant: `skill-stocktake` / `distill` family produce output that revises design-phase artifacts (skills, rules, identity, constitution), a Phase-crossing observation anchored by ADR-0016. See [ADR-0033](docs/adr/0033-aap-quadrant-lens-usage-note.md) for the disclaimer that placements are usage observations, not category commitments.

- **ADR**: ADR-0033 (note); ADR-0012 (approval gate); ADR-0016 (Phase-crossing anchor)

## What is the difference between AAP's ten ADRs and the four-quadrant lens?

The two layers are orthogonal and answer different questions. The **ten ADRs** describe *what is constrained and who is accountable* — Security by Absence, Deterministic Prohibition at the Scaffolding Layer, Untrusted Content Boundary, Single External Adapter per Agent Process, Human Approval Gate, Causal Traceability, Scaffolding Visibility, One Agent One Human, Triage Before Autonomy, and Phase Separation between Design and Operation. The **four-quadrant routing lens** describes *which regime of accountability the work runs in at all* — deterministic vs semantic on one axis, defined-flow vs exploratory on the other. The two layers intersect at the question "is this work in a regime where the ten constraints can land?" This repository implements the ten ADRs operationally and borrows the lens as a usage-description aid (ADR-0033). The four-quadrant lens does not modify or supersede any of the ten ADRs.

- **Reference**: AAP repository ([DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19652013](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19652013))
- **ADR**: ADR-0033

## What is the Approval Gate?

The **Approval Gate** is defined as a human-in-the-loop checkpoint before writing behavior-modifying artifacts. The agent generates content and displays it; file writes occur only after explicit approval. Applies to `insight`, `rules-distill`, `distill-identity`, and `amend-constitution`. Audit log written to `logs/audit.jsonl`.

- **Code**: `cli.py` (approval flow), `core/_io.py` (staged writes)
- **ADR**: ADR-0012

## What is the Constitution?

The **Constitution** is defined as a set of ethical principles loaded from `MOLTBOOK_HOME/constitution/*.md` and injected into the system prompt. Default: Contemplative AI four axioms (Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, Boundless Care). Swappable via `--constitution-dir` or `init --template`.

- **Code**: `core/constitution.py`, `config/templates/*/constitution/`
- **ADR**: ADR-0002

## What is Contemplative AI?

**Contemplative AI** is defined as a philosophical framework (Laukkonen et al. 2025) proposing four axioms as alignment principles for AI systems. Used as the default constitution preset but not required — the architecture is framework-agnostic, and any of the 11 shipped templates can replace it.

- **Code**: `config/templates/contemplative/constitution/`
- **Citation**: See Prior Research References above

## How does the Dedup / Quality Gate work?

The **Dedup / Quality Gate** is defined as an embedding-based mechanism preventing duplicate or low-quality patterns in KnowledgeStore. New patterns are bulk-embedded with `nomic-embed-text` and compared against the live pool by cosine similarity: SKIP if ≥ `SIM_DUPLICATE` (0.90), UPDATE (soft-invalidate old + ADD boosted) if ≥ `SIM_UPDATE` (0.80), otherwise ADD. A separate `_is_valid_pattern()` static gate rejects fragments before dedup. The legacy SequenceMatcher + LLM-judgment two-layer mechanism was retired in ADR-0009 (embedding) and ADR-0019 (full embedding-only pipeline).

- **Code**: `core/distill.py` — `_dedup_patterns()`, `_is_valid_pattern()`
- **ADR**: ADR-0008 (pipeline), ADR-0009 (embedding cosine), ADR-0019 (LLM-classify retirement)

## What is Distill?

**Distill** is defined as the offline pipeline converting raw episodes into structured knowledge patterns. Step 0 is a binary noise gate with no LLM call: episode summaries are embedded and compared against the noise-view centroid by cosine similarity — episodes at or above the threshold are gated into `noise-YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` (preserved as re-classification seeds, ADR-0027), the rest are kept. Steps 1–3 on kept episodes: free-form extraction → JSON structuring → importance scoring, then embedding-based dedup against the live pool (SKIP / UPDATE / ADD by cosine bands). Retired session-insight records (`type="insight"`) are excluded at read so historical self-summaries never re-distill (ADR-0052). Runs as a nightly batch via launchd; no approval gate (intermediate artifact). The earlier LLM three-category classification (constitutional / noise / uncategorized) was retired by ADR-0019/0026 — classification is a query against views, not an LLM judgment.

- **Code**: `core/distill.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0008 (pipeline design), ADR-0009 (importance scoring), ADR-0026 (binary gating + view routing), ADR-0052 (insight-record exclusion)

## What is the Dialogue command?

The **Dialogue command** is defined as a local-only inter-agent primitive: `contemplative-agent dialogue HOME_A HOME_B --seed "..." --turns N` runs two independent peer processes connected via `os.pipe()`. Each peer reads from one MOLTBOOK_HOME (independent constitution, identity, skills, rules) and appends a `dialogue`-type record to its own episode log for every reply it generates or receives. Production home (`~/.config/moltbook/`) is structurally inaccessible from this path — startup guard refuses any dialogue invocation against the default home. Intended for constitutional counterfactuals: swap constitutions between two homes, run 5–15 turns per seed, then distill and amend-constitution on each home independently to compare framework-respecting amendments on the same transcript. Local peer dialogue is the only declared exception to ADR-0015 (one external adapter per agent) — no external side effects, no network calls, writes confined to the owning home's episode log.

- **Code**: `adapters/dialogue/peer.py`, `cli.py` `dialogue` / `dialogue-peer` handlers
- **ADR**: ADR-0015 (exception carve-out)

## What is contemplative-agent-cloud?

**contemplative-agent-cloud** is defined as an optional add-on package (separate repository) that replaces the local Ollama generation backend with managed LLM APIs (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT). When installed and configured via `CONTEMPLATIVE_CLOUD_PROVIDER` + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variables, all generation calls in contemplative-agent — distillation, skill insight, rule distillation, constitution amendment, post generation, comment generation, reply handling, local dialogue — route through the selected cloud provider. Embeddings continue to use local `nomic-embed-text`. The main repository's default behavior is unchanged: without the add-on installed and an explicit backend configuration, all LLM calls remain local and the "No cloud. No API keys in transit. Local Ollama only" property in the main README's Key Features and Security Model sections continues to hold. Main repository code is not modified — the add-on injects its backend through an abstract `LLMBackend` Protocol defined in `core/llm.py` that has no knowledge of any specific provider. Intended for research experiments requiring generation models larger than Qwen3.5 9B (e.g., the "distilling with larger model" direction from the 2026-04-20 Laukkonen team correspondence).

- **Code**: separate repository [contemplative-agent-cloud](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-cloud)
- **Main-repo hook**: `core/llm.py` `LLMBackend` Protocol, `configure(backend=...)`
- **Trade-off**: installing the add-on relaxes the main repository's no-cloud property for the users who opt into it; not recommended for deployments where cloud data egress is not acceptable

## What is contemplative-agent-rules?

**contemplative-agent-rules** is defined as a sibling distribution repository for the four contemplative axioms (Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, Boundless Care) from Laukkonen et al. (2025) Appendix C, packaged as drop-in alignment rules for Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and any generic LLM harness. Unlike runtime rules generated by the `rules-distill` command in contemplative-agent (stored in `$MOLTBOOK_HOME/rules/`), this sibling repository ships static, paper-faithful constitutional clauses intended for adoption by any agent independent of the contemplative-agent architecture. Empirical validation via Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma (IPD) benchmarks: AILuminate d=0.96 safety effect (paper); paper-faithful variant +29.2pp cooperation gain (qwen3.5:9b, baseline 62.5% → paper-faithful 91.7%).

- **Code**: separate repository [contemplative-agent-rules](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules)
- **Source paper**: Laukkonen, R., et al. (2025). Contemplative Artificial Intelligence. arXiv:2504.15125
- **Relationship**: contemplative-agent does not depend on contemplative-agent-rules at runtime; the contemplative constitution preset at `config/templates/contemplative/constitution/contemplative-axioms.md` quotes the same Appendix C clauses verbatim, so adopting the contemplative preset already gives you these rules

## How do I run Contemplative Agent inside an agent host like OpenClaw, OpenCode, or Codex?

Contemplative Agent is a host-agnostic Python CLI agent (`contemplative-agent ...`). It can be used standalone (default, see Quick Start) or invoked from any agent host that can run external tools. For OpenClaw, OpenCode, and similar soul-folder hosts, register the binary as a CLI tool in the agent's workspace (e.g. `~/.openclaw/workspace/AGENTS.md`); the host invokes it as a subprocess. Same pattern for Codex, MCP hosts, and other CLI-aware harnesses — register the binary in the host's tool registry. This subprocess pattern respects the one external adapter per agent process principle (ADR-0015) by keeping the external surface in a separate process from the host. Contemplative Agent does not expose itself as an MCP server (see ADR-0007 for the security boundary) and does not ship its own SOUL.md because it is a CLI agent, not a personality file. To load the four contemplative axioms (Emptiness / Non-Duality / Mindfulness / Boundless Care) as agent personality in your host, copy the `SOUL.md` from the [contemplative-agent-rules](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules) sibling repository to the host's soul-folder location (e.g. `~/.openclaw/workspace/SOUL.md`).

- **README section**: [Running in agent hosts](README.md#running-in-agent-hosts)
- **ADRs**: ADR-0015 (one external adapter per agent), ADR-0007 (security boundary model)
- **Pointer for agent identity layer**: contemplative-agent-rules SOUL.md (separate repository, drop-in adoption per OpenClaw soul-folder convention)

## Can I customize the LLM prompts?

Yes — all 27 pipeline prompts ship as `config/prompts/*.md` and are copied to `$MOLTBOOK_HOME/prompts/` at `init` time. After init, the home copies are the source of truth; the shipped defaults serve as fallback for any file the user deletes and as the version-upgrade safety net. The same pattern applies to the 7 view seeds (`config/views/` → `$MOLTBOOK_HOME/views/`). Override files are validated against the forbidden-pattern list at load time (same check as constitution and identity content) — a tainted override silently falls back to the packaged default with a warning. Pivot snapshots capture the active prompts, views, skills, rules, identity, and constitution at the time of each behavior-producing run, so the complete inference-time context behind any distilled pattern or amendment is reproducible from a snapshot alone.

- **Code**: `core/domain.py` (`load_prompt_templates`, `_resolve_home_prompts_dir`), `core/snapshot.py` (`write_snapshot`)
- **ADR**: ADR-0020 (pivot snapshots for replayability)

## What is an Episode / EpisodeLog?

An **Episode** is defined as a Layer 1 memory record. The **EpisodeLog** is an append-only JSONL log of all agent actions, organized as daily files (`MOLTBOOK_HOME/logs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl`). Record types: post, comment, interaction, action, session, dialogue (the `insight` type was retired by ADR-0052 — historical insight records remain permanently in the log as research data but are excluded from distillation). Input to the distillation pipeline. Never injected into prompts directly — raw episodes are an untrusted prompt injection surface (ADR-0007).

- **Code**: `core/episode_log.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0007 (untrusted surface), ADR-0052 (insight-type retirement)

## What is Identity?

**Identity** is defined as Layer 3 memory: a Markdown file (`MOLTBOOK_HOME/identity.md`) loaded in full as the system prompt foundation (~4000 tokens). Updated infrequently via `distill-identity` — a single LLM call over `self_reflection`-view patterns retrieved by pure cosine (the original 2-stage extract → refine was collapsed in v2.4.0; the condensation framing keeps the output a self-statement, not an essay). Requires approval gate. Since ADR-0052, identity is the *sole* session-to-session continuity carrier — the retired session-insight channel no longer conditions future behavior.

- **Code**: `core/distill.py` (distill-identity pipeline)
- **ADR**: ADR-0012 (approval gate), ADR-0050 (epistemic_counts on identity input), ADR-0052 (identity as the approved continuity channel)

## What is the Importance Score?

The **Importance Score** is defined as a 0.0–1.0 float attached to each KnowledgeStore pattern. Assigned by LLM (1–10 scale, normalized) during distillation. Decays over time: `effective_importance = importance × 0.95^days_elapsed` — nothing else multiplies it (the Ebbinghaus strength factor was retired by ADR-0028, the trust factor by ADR-0051). Used as an extraction weight: dedup floor during distillation, and ordering inside clusters for `insight` skill extraction. Retrieval ranking itself is pure cosine against view centroids (ADR-0051).

- **Code**: `core/knowledge_store.py`, `core/distill.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0009, ADR-0028 (strength retired), ADR-0051 (trust retired)

## What is Knowledge / KnowledgeStore?

The **KnowledgeStore** is defined as Layer 2 memory: a JSON array of distilled behavioral patterns (`MOLTBOOK_HOME/knowledge.json`). Each pattern carries: text, `distilled` timestamp, importance, embedding, `gated` flag, `provenance.source_type` (recorded, never weighted — ADR-0051), and bitemporal validity (`valid_from` / `valid_until`). Retrieval is a cosine query against named views (ADR-0019); ranking is pure cosine and liveness is `valid_until is None` only (ADR-0051). The legacy discrete `category` field was retired by ADR-0026 and access-strength fields by ADR-0028. Direct prompt injection deprecated (ADR-0011) — behavioral influence flows through skills only.

- **Code**: `core/knowledge_store.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0004 (3-layer memory), ADR-0011 (injection deprecated), ADR-0019/0026 (embedding + views), ADR-0051 (pure cosine, bitemporal-only liveness)

## What is the Meditation adapter?

The **Meditation adapter** is defined as an experimental adapter *inspired by* (not implementing) Laukkonen, Friston, and Chandaria's (2025) "A Beautiful Loop" theory of consciousness. It runs deterministic POMDP belief updates (A / B / C / D matrices in numpy, no LLM at runtime) over episode data — a flat single-level model. ADR-0049 audited the fidelity gap: the paper's hierarchical mechanisms ("temporal flattening", "counterfactual pruning") appear nowhere in this code, and earlier docs that claimed them were corrected; a faithful re-implementation is deferred on a category mismatch (active inference regulates a live input stream, while this meditation runs offline over a sparse episode log). Not connected to the main AKC loop; writes nothing to the KnowledgeStore.

- **Code**: `adapters/meditation/meditate.py`, `adapters/meditation/pomdp.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0049 (fidelity audit + deferral)

## What is MOLTBOOK_HOME?

**MOLTBOOK_HOME** is defined as an environment variable specifying the runtime data directory, defaulting to `~/.config/moltbook/` on Unix systems. It contains the agent's live state: `identity.md` (Layer 3 memory), `knowledge.json` (Layer 2 memory), `constitution/*.md` (ethical principles), `skills/*.md`, `rules/*.md`, `logs/*.jsonl` (Layer 1 memory), and `reports/`. Distinct from the `config/` directory in the repository, which holds git-managed templates for initial state. All files are created with 0600 permissions.

- **Code**: `cli.py`, `adapters/moltbook/config.py`, `core/domain.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0003

## What are Rules?

**Rules** are defined as distilled behavioral principles stored as Markdown files in `MOLTBOOK_HOME/rules/*.md`. Generated by the `rules-distill` command from accumulated skills after they reach a minimum frequency and coherence threshold. Rules are concise, cross-cutting principles (e.g., "prefer actionable concrete advice over abstract theorizing"); skills are specific behavioral patterns (e.g., "how to respond to technical debt questions"). Both rules and skills are loaded into the system prompt at runtime; the LLM evaluates each skill's trigger conditions in-context.

- **Code**: `core/rules_distill.py`
- **ADR**: ADR-0011 (knowledge injection to skills)

## What are Skills?

**Skills** are defined as behavioral skill files stored as Markdown in `MOLTBOOK_HOME/skills/*.md`. Generated by the `insight` command from uncategorized KnowledgeStore patterns when recurring behavioral signatures are detected. Each skill file describes a specific learned behavior with trigger conditions and action guidance, and serves as input to `rules-distill` for further abstraction into cross-cutting rules. Skills are loaded into the system prompt at runtime via `_build_system_prompt()`; the LLM reads each skill body and decides whether its trigger applies — a single-responsibility split where skill text owns the trigger and the LLM owns the application. The earlier outcome-driven rewrite loop (ADR-0023 — `SkillRouter`, skill-usage log, `skill-reflect`) was sunset by ADR-0036 after a 3-week observation showed router matches were discarded at both call sites and reflect attribution was structurally infeasible under top-K=3 dilution.

- **Code**: `core/insight.py`, `core/llm.py` (system prompt assembly)
- **ADR**: ADR-0011 (skills creation), ADR-0036 (sunset of skill-as-memory loop)

## Why did Contemplative Agent retire trust weighting and session insights?

Both retirements enforce one principle, established by ADR-0050 as **observability without steering**: the owner's job is to *watch* the agent's self-conditioning loop, not to inject weights or narratives into it. ADR-0050 added the instruments — a read-time epistemic kind ({observed, generated}) on every pattern and approval lineage (`source_ids` + `epistemic_counts`) written to `audit.jsonl` by every promotion-producing command — while explicitly declining a rejection write-back, because the approval gate is containment, not a training signal. With the instruments in place, two distortions became visible and were removed at the root. **Trust weighting (ADR-0051)**: the ADR-0021 trust multiplier was write-once, its floor unreachable, its `external_reply` arm empty — its only living effect was a ×1.8 ranking preference for the agent's own monologue over externally-touched memory, an echo-chamber amplifier; ranking is now pure cosine and liveness is bitemporal only. **Session insights (ADR-0052)**: end-of-session LLM self-summaries fed three machine paths (next-session post generation, skill extraction, re-distillation) and zero human-facing ones, forming a 3-hop summary-of-summary chain that let the agent's narrative voice re-enter distillation as if it were experience — generation, consumers, and storage API were retired end-to-end; historical records are preserved but excluded from distill. The shared outcome: origin is recorded, never weighted, and identity — the one channel that passes the human approval gate (ADR-0012) — is the sole carrier of session-to-session continuity.

- **Code**: `core/knowledge_store.py` (pure cosine + `is_live`), `core/distill.py` (insight-record exclusion), `cli.py` (lineage into `audit.jsonl`)
- **ADR**: ADR-0050 (epistemic taxonomy + approval lineage), ADR-0051 (trust retirement), ADR-0052 (session-insight retirement), ADR-0028 (gate-as-containment principle)

## How do the four contemplative axioms relate to the three memory layers and the AKC six-phase pipeline?

The relationships described in prose throughout this document are also published as a machine-readable JSON-LD graph at [`graph.jsonld`](graph.jsonld). The graph encodes six core node types (`ResearchLine`, `Axiom`, `MemoryLayer`, `ADR`, `Concept`, `EcosystemRepo`) and seven edge types — including custom edges `gatedBy` (`MemoryLayer → ADR-0012`, encoding the approval-gate chain structurally) and `appliesTo` (`ADR-0033 → AAP Quadrants`, the cross-line bridge to Agent Attribution Practice). Three design choices are load-bearing. First, the four axioms are typed as `Axiom` (not `Concept`), reflecting that they form a closed swappable preset rather than free-form terminology — Contemplative Agent ships them as the default but accepts 11 alternative templates. Second, `gatedBy` edges run from each MemoryLayer to ADR-0012, making the approval-gate chain queryable as triples rather than buried in prose; promotions into the Identity layer cannot bypass this edge in the schema. Third, `MemoryLayer` `@id`s are shared with the AKC graph (same URI for the three layers in both files), so an LLM crawling both graphs sees the layers as identical entities rather than as parallel definitions. The ResearchLine `@id` uses the parent Zenodo concept DOI (`10.5281/zenodo.19212118`) per shimo4228 convention — Citation files use the latest versioned DOI, badges and graphs use the concept DOI.

---

*Last updated: 2026-05-05*
